Base directory for this skill: d:\PROJECTS\freddie-ai\.claude\skills\create-track

Create a Suno AI track.

**"Jazz celtic molodies, soft shamizen single pluck, warm rhodes, soft slow harp single pluck, soft snare drums, just woke up morning vibe, barely wake up, drinking coffee vibe, slow but not sleepy, off-beat, no high pitch, all lowered picth"**

## Input Collection

Before any analysis, collect these 6 inputs from the user. If `"Jazz celtic molodies, soft shamizen single pluck, warm rhodes, soft slow harp single pluck, soft snare drums, just woke up morning vibe, barely wake up, drinking coffee vibe, slow but not sleepy, off-beat, no high pitch, all lowered picth"` already contains answers (inline or structured), parse them. Otherwise, present the questionnaire:

```
1. Type: [bgm / song]
2. Lyrics: [path to lyrics file / none]
3. Bracket Format: [pipe / stack] (default: pipe)
4. Prompt: [describe the track — genre, mood, instruments, scene, energy]
5. Music Card: [name / path / none / skip]
6. Archetype: [name / path / none / skip]
```

**Parse rules:**
- **Type** — `bgm` = instrumental/background music (all lines bracketed, `[Instrumental]` + `[No Vocals]`). `song` = vocal track with lyrics. Stored in output frontmatter as `type: "instrumental"` (bgm) or `type: "vocal"` (song).
- **Lyrics** — three options:
  - **File path** — path to a lyrics `.md` file (from `/lyrics` output). Lyrics used as-is with platform formatting added.
  - **Inline text** — user pastes full lyrics directly (multi-line). Lyrics used as-is with platform formatting added.
  - **`none` or blank** — generate lyrics in P3-005. If `song` type: Tala writes vocal lyrics. If `bgm` type: Tala writes structural brackets only.
- **Bracket Format** — controls how `[Instrument:]` tags are written in the Lyrics block. Default: `pipe`.
  - **`pipe`** (default) — all instruments on one line separated by `|`. Example: `[Instrument: Shamisen melodic plucks | Harp low arpeggios | Brushed snare groove]`
  - **`stack`** — each instrument on its own line. Example: `[Instrument: Shamisen melodic plucks]` / `[Instrument: Harp low arpeggios]` / `[Instrument: Brushed snare groove]`
- **Prompt** — the creative direction. Genre, mood, instruments, scene, energy. This drives the entire analysis.
- **Music Card** — three options:
  - **Name or path** — use this specific card (override). Provides hard BPM/key constraints and Suno Translation as starting point.
  - **`none`** — no preference. Tala may match from discover and use as loose reference.
  - **`skip`** — explicitly don't use any music card. Tala ignores all discover matches.
- **Archetype** — three options:
  - **Name or path** — use this specific archetype (override). Shapes the sonic character.
  - **`none`** — no preference. Tala may match from discover and use as reference.
  - **`skip`** — explicitly don't use any archetype. Tala ignores all discover matches.

**Example inputs:**

User runs: `/create-track`
Freddie asks the 6 questions. User answers:
```
1. bgm
2. none
3. pipe
4. Jazzhop, warm shamisen, warm harp, slow morning, morning coffee vibe
5. skip
6. skip
```

**Echo back** the parsed inputs before proceeding to Phase 1:
```
Type:           bgm (→ instrumental)
Lyrics:         none (will generate structural brackets)
Bracket Format: pipe
Prompt:         Jazzhop, warm shamisen, warm harp, slow morning, morning coffee vibe
Music Card:     skip (ignored)
Archetype:      skip (ignored)
```

Pass all 6 inputs to the Tala sub-agent in Phase 1.

## Execution Model

**Freddie orchestrates, Tala executes.** This skill runs inline in the main conversation. Freddie handles all user interaction and review gates. Tala is dispatched as a sub-agent for bounded analysis and generation work.

| Phase | Runs in | Purpose |
|-------|---------|---------|
| 1 — Analysis | Tala sub-agent | Dissect prompt, discover refs, health checks |
| 2 — Analysis Review | Freddie (main) | Present summary table, user approves/adjusts |
| 3 — Generation | Tala sub-agent | Build Style/Lyrics, phonetics validation, run tri-critic, track names |
| 4 — Generation Review | Freddie (main) | Present critic digest + output, user approves/adjusts |
| 5 — Save | Freddie (main) | Write single .md file to disk, reflect |

## Workflow

Follow the mermaid diagram below. Each node `[PX-NNN]` maps to a section in `workflow.md` with full instructions.

```mermaid
graph TD
    INPUT[Input Collection: Type, Lyrics, Prompt, Music Card, Archetype] --> P1

    subgraph P1["Phase 1 — Analysis (Tala sub-agent)"]
        P1_001[P1-001: Parse structured inputs] --> P1_002[P1-002: Query brain.db]
        P1_002 --> P1_003[P1-003: Discover references]
        P1_003 --> P1_004[P1-004: Normalize genres]
        P1_004 --> P1_005[P1-005: Distill + health checks]
        P1_005 --> P1_006[P1-006: Vibe map translation]
    end

    P1 --> P2

    subgraph P2["Phase 2 — Analysis Review (Freddie)"]
        P2_001[P2-001: Format analysis table] --> P2_002{P2-002: User choice}
        P2_002 -->|approve| P3
        P2_002 -->|adjust| P1
        P2_002 -->|retry| P1
        P2_002 -->|cancel| DONE[Done]
    end

    subgraph P3["Phase 3 — Generation (Tala sub-agent)"]
        P3_001[P3-001: Load matched refs] --> P3_002[P3-002: Build Style block]
        P3_002 --> P3_003[P3-003: Charcount validation]
        P3_003 --> P3_004[P3-004: Build Exclude Style]
        P3_004 --> P3_005[P3-005: Write Lyrics block]
        P3_005 --> P3_006[P3-006: Vocal performance pass]
        P3_006 --> P3_007[P3-007: Set W/SI values]
        P3_007 --> P3_007b[P3-007b: Prompt validation gate]
        P3_007b --> P3_008[P3-008: Tri-critic loop]
        P3_008 --> P3_009[P3-009: Reconcile critic feedback]
        P3_009 --> P3_010[P3-010: Generate track names]
    end

    P3 --> P4

    subgraph P4["Phase 4 — Generation Review (Freddie)"]
        P4_001[P4-001: Format generation output] --> P4_002{P4-002: User choice}
        P4_002 -->|approve| P5
        P4_002 -->|adjust| P3
        P4_002 -->|retry| P3
        P4_002 -->|"test in Suno"| P4_001
        P4_002 -->|cancel| DONE
    end

    subgraph P5["Phase 5 — Save (Freddie)"]
        P5_001[P5-001: Compliance gate] --> P5_002[P5-002: Write file to disk]
        P5_002 --> P5_003[P5-003: Reflect + consolidate + brain sync]
    end

    P5 --> DONE
```

### Sub-agent dispatch

Use the **Agent tool** with `subagent_type: "tala"` for Phase 1 and Phase 3. Each dispatch is a bounded task — Tala returns structured results, never writes to disk.

**Phase 1 sub-agent prompt must include:**
- All 6 structured inputs (type, lyrics, bracket format, prompt, music card, archetype)
- Instructions: run discover, query brain.db, parse and normalize genres, distill ingredients, health checks, vibe map translation
- Return format: structured analysis (type, genre, mood, instruments, BPM, key, music card, archetype, lyrics status, frequency map, explanations)

**Phase 3 sub-agent prompt must include:**
- Locked analysis from Phase 2 (the approved table)
- Any user adjustments applied
- Instructions: load refs, build Style (~75-120 chars), charcount validate, build Exclude Style, write Lyrics (typed brackets), vocal pass if vocal, set W/SI, run tri-critic (Gemini → MiniMax → Grok), generate 18 track names
- Return format: Style, Exclude Style, W/SI + justification, Lyrics, critic outputs (raw flags + digest), track names (all 18), reconciliation decisions, production notes

## Phase 1 — Analysis

Dispatch Tala sub-agent. Follow nodes P1-001 through P1-006 in `workflow.md`.

Tala returns a structured analysis covering:
- Genre(s) — normalized, max 2, with family grouping
- Mood — translated from user vibes to production-safe descriptors
- Instruments — lead/support/rhythm with register and role
- BPM — within genre range, sweet spot identified
- Key — matches mood (minor/major alignment)
- Music Card — matched or none, with measured constraints if matched
- Archetype — matched or none, with subversion notes if applicable
- Frequency map — no collisions between instruments
- Health check results — pass/fail per channel

## Phase 2 — Analysis Review

Present the analysis as a summary table:

```
| Dimension   | Value              | Source        | Explanation                |
|-------------|--------------------|---------------|----------------------------|
| Type        | bgm / song         | user input    | ...                        |
| Lyrics      | provided / generate| user input    | {path if provided}         |
| Genre       | ...                | user/tala/ref | ...                        |
| Mood        | ...                | user/tala/ref | ...                        |
| Instruments | ...                | user/tala/ref | ...                        |
| BPM         | ...                | tala/ref/card | ...                        |
| Key         | ...                | tala/ref/card | ...                        |
| Music Card  | none / {name}      | user/discover | ...                        |
| Archetype   | none / {name}      | user/discover | ...                        |
```

Show any health check warnings or notable decisions below the table.

Ask user:
```
[1] Approve  [2] Adjust  [3] Retry  [4] Cancel
```

- **Approve** → Phase 3
- **Adjust** → collect user modifications, re-run Phase 1 with adjustments appended to prompt
- **Retry** → re-run Phase 1 from scratch (fresh analysis, no adjustments)
- **Cancel** → abort, done

## Phase 3 — Generation

Dispatch Tala sub-agent with locked analysis. Follow nodes P3-001 through P3-010 in `workflow.md`.

Tala builds the track and returns:
- Style block (~75-120 chars, charcount validated)
- Exclude Style (minimal with typed brackets)
- W/SI values with per-value justification
- Lyrics (typed brackets, piping, layer lines)
- Tri-critic results:
  - Gemini: `production-musical` critic flags + what was accepted/rejected
  - MiniMax: `production-effectiveness` critic flags + what was accepted/rejected
  - Grok: wild-card flags + what was accepted/rejected
- 18 track names with model tags and strategy labels
- Production notes (char count, element count, frequency map, slot validation)

## Phase 4 — Generation Review

Present results in this order:

**1. Critic Digest Table:**
```
| Critic  | Flags | Accepted | Key Changes                          |
|---------|-------|----------|--------------------------------------|
| Gemini  | N     | N/N      | ...                                  |
| MiniMax | N     | N/N      | ...                                  |
| Grok    | N     | N/N      | ...                                  |
```

**2. Per-critic narrative** — 2-3 sentences each: what was flagged, what was accepted, what was rejected and why.

**3. Track Summary Table** — same format as Phase 2, now with final post-critic values.

**4. Generated Output:**

Style:
```
{style block}
```

Exclude Style:
```
{exclude style}
```

Advanced Settings:
- **Weirdness**: X% — {justification}
- **Style Influence**: X% — {justification}

Lyrics:
```
{full lyrics with typed brackets}
```

**5. Track Names Table** — all 18 with model tags + strategy labels:
```
| # | Name | Model | Strategy |
|---|------|-------|----------|
| 1 | {name — this becomes the title + filename} | {model} | {strategy} |
| ... | ... | ... | ... |
| 18 | ... | ... | ... |
```

**6. Production Notes** — char count, element count, frequency map, BPM rationale, exclude rationale, W/SI justification.

**7. Cost Summary** — show API usage from this phase:
```
=== Phase 3 Usage ===
Gemini:  X in / Y out | Jazz celtic molodies, soft shamizen single pluck, warm rhodes, soft slow harp single pluck, soft snare drums, just woke up morning vibe, barely wake up, drinking coffee vibe, slow but not sleepy, off-beat, no high pitch, all lowered picth.00 (N calls)
MiniMax: X in / Y out | Jazz celtic molodies, soft shamizen single pluck, warm rhodes, soft slow harp single pluck, soft snare drums, just woke up morning vibe, barely wake up, drinking coffee vibe, slow but not sleepy, off-beat, no high pitch, all lowered picth.00 (N calls)
Grok:    X in / Y out | Jazz celtic molodies, soft shamizen single pluck, warm rhodes, soft slow harp single pluck, soft snare drums, just woke up morning vibe, barely wake up, drinking coffee vibe, slow but not sleepy, off-beat, no high pitch, all lowered picth.00 (N calls)
Total:   Jazz celtic molodies, soft shamizen single pluck, warm rhodes, soft slow harp single pluck, soft snare drums, just woke up morning vibe, barely wake up, drinking coffee vibe, slow but not sleepy, off-beat, no high pitch, all lowered picth.00
```

Ask user:
```
[1] Approve  [2] Adjust  [3] Retry  [4] Cancel
```

- **Approve** → Phase 5
- **Adjust** → collect user modifications, re-run Phase 3 with adjustments
- **Retry** → re-run Phase 3 from scratch (new generation, same locked analysis)
- **Cancel** → abort, done

User may also say "let me test in Suno first" — wait for feedback, then re-present or adjust based on real-world results.

## Phase 5 — Save

1. Run `getdate` to get folderDate and folderTime
2. Use track name #1 as filename slug (lowercase, hyphenated)
3. Write single `.md` file to: `vault/studio/tracks/{folderDate}/{folderTime}-{track-name-slug}.md`
4. Use the template in `assets/track-output-template.md` for file format
5. Run `reflect` for learning capture + brain.db decision logging
6. If reflect returns `consolidationRecommended: true` → run `consolidate-memory --agent tala`
7. Sync brain.db: `brain.ts --check`
8. Print usage report (Gemini/MiniMax/Grok tokens + cost)
9. Print learning summary (learnings saved, decisions logged, consolidation status)

## Output

A single `.md` file at `vault/studio/tracks/YYYY-MM-DD/HHMMSS-{track-name}.md` containing:
- Frontmatter (id, title, genre, mood, bpm, key, platform, type, tags)
- Original prompt
- Analysis table
- Style, Exclude Style, W/SI with justification
- Lyrics with typed brackets
- Critic digest (table + per-critic narrative)
- All 18 track names with model tags + strategy labels
- Production notes

## Compliance Gate

Before writing to disk, verify:

1. Style block: ~75-120 chars, validated via `charcount`
2. Exclude Style: minimal/empty with typed brackets, or focused excludes
3. W/SI: values with justification
4. Lyrics: typed brackets per section, correct format for track type
5. Track names: exactly 18 with model tags + strategy labels
6. Tri-critic: all three ran — no skipping
7. User approved in Phase 4

content.text:
Now I have everything I need. Let me compile the full Phase 1 analysis.

---

## P1-001: Parse Structured Inputs

**Type:** BGM (instrumental) -- all lines bracketed, `[Instrumental]` at start, `[No Vocals]` at end.

**Lyrics:** None provided. Will generate structural brackets in Phase 3 (Path B).

**Bracket Format:** Pipe -- all instruments on one `[Instrument:]` line separated by `|`.

**Music Card:** Skip (user explicit). All discover matches ignored.

**Archetype:** Skip (user explicit). All discover matches ignored.

**Extracted from prompt:**

| Element | Extracted | Notes |
|---------|-----------|-------|
| Genres | Jazz, Celtic | Two distinct genre families -- fusion territory |
| Instruments | Shamisen (single pluck), Rhodes (warm), Harp (soft slow single pluck), Snare drums (soft) | Four instruments named -- over budget by one |
| Mood/Vibe | "just woke up morning vibe", "barely awake", "drinking coffee vibe" | Scene-based, needs translation |
| Energy | "slow but not sleepy" | Wants gentle momentum, not dragging |
| Rhythm | "off-beat" | Syncopated feel |
| Pitch constraint | "no high pitch, all lowered pitch" | Everything in lower registers |
| BPM | Not specified, implied slow | |
| Key | Not specified | |

**Flags raised during parse:**
- Four instruments named (shamisen, rhodes, harp, snare) -- max is 3. One must be cut or merged into texture.
- "Jazz celtic melodies" -- user says celtic as a melodic flavor, not a genre. This means celtic melody character applied through instrument choice (harp), not a genre tag.
- Shamisen + harp both described as "single pluck" -- potential role collision if both play sparse melody.

---

## P1-002 / P1-003: Brain.db + Discovery Results

**What came back and what matters:**

1. **Soft Brush Taps music card** -- Our own past track. Shamisen lead + harp + brush texture, jazz lounge, E Minor, 63 BPM. Extremely relevant as a near-sibling, but user said skip music cards. I will note the learnings (jazz lounge causes bleed, harp in low arpeggios works, brush taps not brushed snare) but not use its Profile as hard constraints.

2. **Frequency Register table** -- Critical for this build:
   - Shamisen: high-mid (4-8 kHz) -- user wants it lowered
   - Rhodes: low-mid (250 Hz-1 kHz) -- already low, good
   - Harp (low strings): low-mid (250 Hz-1 kHz) -- collision with Rhodes
   - Harp (high strings): high-mid (4-8 kHz) -- collision with shamisen

Run the full Phase 3 workflow:

### P3-001: Load matched refs

### P3-002: Build Style block
Build a Style block targeting ~75-120 chars. Jazz is the only genre tag. Mood descriptors carry the warmth/morning feel. Do NOT include instrument names in Style — instruments go in Lyrics brackets only. Do NOT use "Celtic" in Style. Do NOT use "Jazz Lounge" (causes instrument bleed per past decision). Do NOT use "Lo-fi" (Jazz already pulls lo-fi via gravity).

### P3-003: Charcount validation
Run `bun src/tools/charcount.ts "{style block}"` to validate char count is in 75-120 range. If outside range, adjust and re-validate.

### P3-004: Build Exclude Style
Minimal excludes with typed brackets. Focus on what could go wrong: Celtic genre gravity instruments (fiddle, tin whistle, bodhran), high-pitch elements, vocals, bright/aggressive qualities.

### P3-005: Write Lyrics block (BGM Path B)
Generate structural brackets for instrumental BGM. Pipe format for instrument lines. Structure:
- `[Instrumental]` at top
- Section headers: `[Intro]`, `[Verse]`, `[Bridge]`, `[Chorus]`, `[Outro]` etc.
- `[Instrument: X | Y | Z]` lines — pipe-separated, one line per section
- `[Tempo: ...]`, `[Key: ...]`, `[Dynamics: ...]` tags as needed
- `[No Vocals]` at end
- Each instrument entry includes role + behavior: e.g., "Shamisen melodic single plucks" not just "Shamisen"

Instruments per section should vary — not every instrument plays in every section. Build energy arc: gentle intro → slightly more active middle → gentle outro.

### P3-006: Vocal performance pass
SKIP — this is BGM, no vocals.

### P3-007: Set W/SI values
- **Weirdness (W):** Set percentage with justification. Jazz + non-standard instruments (shamisen) benefits from some weirdness for fusion flavor, but too much breaks the gentle vibe.
- **Style Influence (SI):** Set percentage with justification. Higher SI keeps the Style block dominant (good for clear genre signal), lower allows more Lyrics bracket influence.

### P3-007b: Prompt validation gate
Run the validate-prompt tool: `bun src/tools/validate-prompt.ts --style "{style}" --lyrics "{lyrics}" --exclude "{exclude}" --type instrumental`
Fix any errors. Warnings must be resolved or justified.

### P3-008: Tri-critic loop
Run all three critics. Use the critic prompt files in `.claude/critics/`:

1. **Gemini critic** (`production-musical`): `bun src/tools/gemini.ts --critic production-musical --style "{style}" --lyrics "{lyrics}" --exclude "{exclude}" --type instrumental --bpm 82 --key "Eb Major"`
2. **MiniMax critic** (`production-effectiveness`): `bun src/tools/gemini.ts --critic production-effectiveness --style "{style}" --lyrics "{lyrics}" --exclude "{exclude}" --type instrumental --bpm 82 --key "Eb Major"`
3. **Grok critic** (wild-card): `bun src/tools/gemini.ts --critic wild-card --style "{style}" --lyrics "{lyrics}" --exclude "{exclude}" --type instrumental --bpm 82 --key "Eb Major"`

For each critic, record: flags raised, what you accept, what you reject and why.

### P3-009: Reconcile critic feedback
Apply accepted changes. Re-run charcount if Style changed. Document what changed and why.

### P3-010: Generate track names
Run: `bun src/tools/tracknames.ts --style "{final style}" --lyrics "{final lyrics}" --mood "warm intimate morning gentle" --genre "jazz"`
This generates 18 names (6 per model) with model tags and strategy labels. Return ALL 18 — do not curate or reduce.

## Return format

Return ALL of the following:

1. **Style block** (final, post-critic, charcount validated)
2. **Exclude Style** (final, post-critic)
3. **W/SI values** with per-value justification

8. **Cost summary** — Gemini/MiniMax/Grok token usage and costs from this phase

IMPORTANT: Do NOT write any files to disk. Return everything as structured text. Freddie handles file writing in Phase 5.

## PHASE 3 GENERATION OUTPUT

---

### 1. Style Block (final, post-critic, charcount validated)

```

**Rationale:** Fiddle/Tin Whistle/Bodhran block Celtic genre gravity instruments that harp could pull in. Vocals/Singing is belt-and-suspenders for vocal leakage alongside `[Instrumental]`/`[No Vocals]`. Bright/Aggressive prevent tonal drift from the soft, warm palette.

---

### 3. W/SI Values with Justification

**Weirdness: 35%**
Jazz with non-standard instruments (shamisen) benefits from some creative latitude for the fusion to feel organic rather than mechanical. But the gentle, intimate mood demands predictability — no wild harmonic detours that break the morning quiet. 35% is in the "safe zone" per the reference guide, giving Suno just enough room to find interesting phrasing without breaking structure. Lower than the 50% default because this is background music — surprises are unwelcome in BGM.

**Style Influence: 65%**
Higher SI keeps Jazz as the dominant genre signal, which matters here because shamisen and harp could confuse Suno without a strong genre anchor. 65% is high enough to lock the swing feel and jazz harmonic language, but not so high (80%+) that it overrides the typed bracket instructions. The detailed `[Instrument:]` brackets need room to influence the output — SI at 65% balances genre purity with bracket responsiveness.

**Energy arc:** Low (intro, shamisen alone) -> Low-Medium (verse, shamisen + harp) -> Medium (chorus, full trio) -> Low-Medium (verse, trio thinning) -> Low (bridge, harp leads, shamisen sparse) -> Medium (chorus, full trio) -> Fading (shamisen + harp only, slowing)

**MiniMax (Production-Effectiveness):** Predictability score 4/5. Caught three actionable issues: "warm" overuse across Lyrics (reduced from 6 to 3 direct uses via synonym rotation), "soft dynamics" risking literal volume reduction (changed to "natural dynamics"), and "celtic modal phrasing" triggering culture-genre bleed (changed to "modal phrasing"). Rejected the suggestion to remove `[No Vocals]` from Lyrics — this is a hard rule for vocal leakage prevention per platform knowledge. Also rejected dropping `[Instrumental]` reinforcement.

**Grok (Wild-Card):** Validated the lean Style + rich typed-bracket approach. Agreed on `[Outro]` reliability issue but suggested `[Big Finish]` which contradicts `[Energy: Fading]` — rejected. Suggested adding upright bass to Choruses — rejected because the locked analysis already cut Rhodes for frequency collision with harp in the same register neighborhood, and adding a 4th instrument violates the 3-instrument max. Suggested adding "analog warmth, soft room reverb, rolled-off highs" to Style — rejected because it would push Style over char budget and "rolled-off highs" applies only to shamisen (per locked analysis), not globally.

`/bye` is the session closer — the counterpart to `/morning`. Triggers reflection, syncs brain.db, consolidates memory, and logs to the daily note.

Raw context capture is handled automatically by the Stop hook (`capture-raw.ts`). No manual capture needed.

For formal dev sessions with task management, use `/dev-end` instead.

## Execution Model

**Freddie handles everything inline.** No sub-agents.

## Workflow

### Phase 1 — Reflect

Identify session metadata:
- **Agents involved** — freddie, mccall, wick, tala, etc.
- **Skill/workflow** — what was the session about
- **Summary** — one-line summary (use  if provided)
- **Outcome** — success, partial, or failed

```bash
bun src/tools/reflect.ts --agent freddie --task "{summary}" --workflow {skill} --outcome {success/partial/failed} --trace "{brief trace}"
```

### Phase 2 — Sync & Consolidate

```bash
bun src/tools/brain.ts --check
```

Check if consolidation is needed:
```bash
bun src/tools/consolidate-memory.ts --list
```

If any agent has 3+ inbox items, run:
```bash
bun src/tools/consolidate-memory.ts --auto
```

### Phase 3 — Daily Log

Log the session to today's daily note:

```bash
bun src/tools/obsidian/sections.ts add-log --content "Session: {summary}. Agents: {list}."
```

### Phase 4 — Sign Off

Print:
```
Session saved.
  Reflect: {learnings} learnings, {decisions} decisions
  Consolidation: {ran/not needed}
  Daily: logged

See you next time.
```
